sound_type: pup group yipping -- high thin staccato 'yip-yip-yip'. 800-2,500 Hz. Play, practice, sibling bonding. E=4
ecosystem: Den site. Hillside burrow. Spring (April-June). Pups emerge at 2-3 weeks.
season: Spring (April-June). Pup emergence. Yipping peaks 4-8 weeks.

coordinates.description: Coyote pup yipping at den sites across urban and suburban Puget Sound lowlands.

taxonomy_note.description: Second Coyote entry. Adult-pup pair (208-209). Second Canidae. Canidae quartet begins (208-211).
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Canidae: Coyote Howl (208, E=5), Coyote Pup Yipping (209, E=4).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Canis latrans = 'barking dog'. latrans from Latin 'to bark'.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Canis -- the dog genus. Coyote is the most adaptable member.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Canidae: second entry. Adult-pup pair. Quartet begins.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Urban-adapted carnivore. Den-based pup rearing.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 800-2,500 Hz. Higher than adult howl by roughly half an octave.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Pup yip: 800-2,500 Hz. Pup distress: 1,000-3,000 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Pup yip: 0.1-0.3 sec per yip. Bouts 10-60 seconds.
song_metrics.note_structure: Short staccato yips in rapid succession. Group chorus overlapping.
song_metrics.note_types: Play yip (staccato, high), distress whine (sustained, higher), greeting frenzy (rapid, excited).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Rapid staccato bursts. Multiple pups overlapping. Chaotic group chorus.
song_metrics.timbre: High, thin, staccato. Small body cavity. Small vocal cords. Enthusiastic chaos.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Group chorus of small voices. Enthusiastic but contained. 50-200 m carry.
song_metrics.tempo: Rapid-fire. Yips every 0.3-0.5 seconds during play. Faster during excitement.
song_metrics.context: Den emergence. Sibling play. Adult greeting. Social hierarchy establishment. Spring.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal variation in pup yipping across populations.
song_metrics.key_property: PUP YIPPING. High thin staccato. 800-2,500 Hz. E=4. ADULT-PUP PAIR (208-209). Second Canidae. Den nursery. Canidae quartet begins. v1.49.350.

pelage.overall: Pup: grayish-brown fuzzy coat, darker than adult. 250-300 g at birth, growing rapidly. Ears initially floppy, standing up at 3-4 weeks.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Small grayish pups at den entrance. Multiple pups playing together. Adults nearby or approaching with food.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Born with fuzzy natal coat. Develops adult-like pelage through summer. By fall, resembles small adult.

physical_description.overall: Pup at birth: 250-300 g. By 8 weeks: 2-5 kg. Grayish-brown fuzzy coat. Floppy ears becoming erect. Litter of 4-7.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Group of small grayish-brown pups at burrow entrance on hillside. Adults coming and going. Play-fighting, yipping.
physical_description.field_id: Multiple small grayish animals playing at den entrance on hillside. High-pitched yipping. Adults returning with food. Spring. Observe from distance -- do not approach den.

nodes.title: Adult-Pup Pair -- 208-209
nodes.description: Coyote howl (E=5, territory) and pup yipping (E=4, play). The -1 infant step across consecutive degrees. Four families confirmed.

nodes.title: Den as Provisioned Nursery
nodes.description: Underground birth. Hillside burrow. Both parents provision. Helpers contribute. The den is the warm center of the canid family.

nodes.title: Vocal Development -- Yip to Howl
nodes.description: Pup yips lengthen and deepen over 8-16 weeks. Transition tracks physical growth. By fall, juveniles produce recognizable howls.

nodes.title: Cross-Family Infant Pattern -- Four Families
nodes.description: Cervidae, Ursidae, Bovidae, Canidae. The infant is always -1 energy. Pattern confirmed across four mammalian families.

cross_references.relationship: ADULT-PUP PAIR! Howl (E=5, territory) to yip (E=4, play). The -1 infant step. Howl communicates; yip rehearses.
CANIDAE QUARTET! Next canid: the wolf. E=7. The most powerful voice. Extirpated, returning.
INFANT PARALLEL! Kid mew (E=3, alpine) and pup yip (E=4, den). Both infant voices, both -1 from adult.
CIRCLE OF WARMTH! Campfire (human gathering) and den (canid gathering). Both warmed by community.
